mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate raises equity valuations through a lower discount rate and easier credit conditions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the policy path is clearly easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in RSAFS above trend signals stronger current consumption demand and revenue growth for listed firms, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show a positive average 20-day SPX return relative to untriggered periods, or if RSAFS crossovers fail to improve hit rate above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates slowing labor income and weaker household spending, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment shock episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial production and manufacturing momentum, which should support near-term equity returns by improving earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO do not correspond to positive 5-day forward SPX returns more often than chance, or if the effect disappears after accounting for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and GDPC1 both rise, housing activity and aggregate demand are expanding together, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through stronger broad-based growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition HOUST up and GDPC1 up does not outperform either series alone in explaining 60-day forward SPX returns, or if combined-trigger instances do not show positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and a FEDFUNDS rate that stays below inflation indicates negative real policy rates, which supports positive S&P 500 returns as financial conditions remain accommodative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains below CPIAUCSL year-over-year pace
failure_condition: Reject if periods with negative estimated real rates do not show higher 60-day SPX returns than neutral or positive-real-rate periods, or if the interaction term is not stable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration phase, a simultaneous rise in UNRATE and slowdown in PAYEMS signals a coming earnings downdraft and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration condition does not yield negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is not stronger than using UNRATE or PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led slowdown, falling HOUST combined with weak RSAFS reflects cooling demand and fading revenue momentum, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month average and RSAFS growth is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-retail weakness condition fails to produce lower 60-day SPX returns than the sample baseline, or if positive returns dominate triggered cases.
